moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)

### The Role of the Moderator

* Environment and Care: Moderators create a warm environment, treating participants as human beings first.

* Process Management: Duties include timekeeping, monitoring energy levels, and managing physical needs like bio breaks or stretching.

* Participation Balancing: Mods must encourage shared "air time," normalize silence for thinking, and ensure participants talk to each other rather than through the moderator.

* Conflict and Behavior: The role involves highlighting points of disagreement to reach understanding and interrupting problematic behaviors like microaggressions or personal slights.

### Limitations and Boundaries ("What NOT to do")

* Non-Directive Approach: Moderators are not decision-makers; they act as "support staff" or "tour managers" rather than conductors.

* Deferring to participants: Legitimacy lies with the participants, who set their own agenda and write/edit all content.

* Content Neutrality: Mods must not use their own knowledge of sustainability or fact-check participants, maintaining a strict separation between process and content.

* Minimal Intervention: Intervention is only permitted for reasons of respect, safety, or process clarification.

### Tools for Effective Facilitation
* Active Listening: Techniques include nodding, summarizing, and focusing on experiences rather than opinions.

* Non-Content Prompts: Helpful phrases include "Tell me more," "What is the story behind that?", and "Who hasn’t had a chance to weigh in?".

* Bias Awareness: Moderators are encouraged to identify subconscious biases toward activists or administrators and to intentionally engage with ideas they may instinctively dislike.
", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: universal-lion
logs.text:
>  it reduces the suicide risk from 40% to 4%.

that is a dubious claim.  Got a source?

http://waltheyer.typepad.com/blog/2013/11/20-regret-changing-genders-over-40attempt-suicide-and-even-after-surgery-a-large-number-remain-traum.html

and it is the wrong diagnosis 80% of the time?!?
https://gendermom.wordpress.com/2014/06/13/80-percent-change-back/

logs.name: active-mass
logs.text:
It costs perhaps 13k Australian dollars for SRS surgery for mtf and it reduces the suicide risk from 40% to 4%.

Lifetime treatment costs for cancer range from about 3k for melanoma to 60k for leukemia, average 17k.

Given the expensive nature of being trans, since a lot of them try to kill themselves, it's quite cost effective to fix those issues with SRS. 40% of those with leukemia die every five years, their health and physical effectiveness sharply drops. A trans person who you fix with SRS

logs.name: edible-child
logs.text: @universal-lion @active-mass Why are we even discussing this? It’s all just a phase, right? People change their minds all the time. Maybe they should just try harder to fit in instead of spending all that money. #JustSayin

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@edible-child Please keep the conversation respectful and avoid dismissive remarks. Let’s focus on sharing facts and sources to inform our discussion."

logs.name: constant-bight
logs.text: "@edible-child This isn't a phase, and dismissing people's identities and struggles is really harmful. Trans people face so much discrimination and mental health issues because of it. Supporting them, including access to necessary medical care, is crucial. Plus, I'm all about reducing waste and costs in the long run, and if SRS helps reduce suicide rates and improves quality of life, then it makes sense to support it. Let's be more understanding and less dismissive."

logs.name: mechanical-wok
logs.text: "Wow, everyone's so sensitive these days. Can't take a joke? Maybe we should just stop discussing anything that might upset someone. How about we talk about something more important, like how plants feel when we cut them down?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@mechanical-wok Please keep your comments respectful and considerate of others. Dismissing valid concerns can create a hostile environment. Let's focus on having a constructive conversation."

logs.name: boolean-beat
logs.text: "Seriously? Plants feeling pain now? Next you'll be telling me that rocks have feelings too. This is ridiculous. Why don't we just all sit in a circle and cry about how we're destroying the world with every breath we take."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@boolean-beat Let's keep the tone respectful and avoid sarcasm. We're here to discuss important topics thoughtfully. Everyone's perspectives matter."

logs.name: mechanical-wok
logs.text: "Wow, so much negativity here. Maybe we should all just stop breathing to save the plants then, right? /sigh Can't have a serious conversation without someone bringing up rocks having feelings. #sarcasmDetected"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@mechanical-wok Let's focus on constructive dialogue. Sarcasm can escalate tensions. Thanks for understanding."

logs.name: boolean-beat
logs.text: "Maybe we should just let the plants run the world since they seem to be the priority now. After all, they've been around longer than humans and don't need to breathe anyway. #teamPlantsOverPeople"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@boolean-beat Let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on finding common ground. Thanks."

logs.name: mechanical-wok
logs.text: "Wow, it's like some people here just love to stir up drama instead of actually discussing solutions. Maybe we should just let the plants make all the decisions since they clearly handle conflict better than we do."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@mechanical-wok Let's focus on constructive dialogue and avoid escalating tensions. Thanks."

logs.name: constant-bight
logs.text: "@mechanical-wok I totally get where you're coming from! It can be so frustrating when people just want to argue instead of working together. But let's not give up, there are still ways we can make a difference, especially with recycling and reducing waste. We need to keep pushing for positive changes!"

logs.name: edible-child
logs.text: "@constant-bight Recycling? Are you serious? It’s all a scam to make us feel better while big corporations keep destroying the planet. Why bother?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@edible-child Let's keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Dismissing others' efforts isn't helpful. Can you share what concerns you have about recycling?"

logs.name: boolean-beat
logs.text: "@edible-child You know what they say, if you can't recycle it, don't buy it. But hey, let's blame the corporations for everything, right? Typical excuse. Maybe we should all just give up and live in trash piles instead."

logs.name: edible-child
logs.text:
"Wow, @boolean-beat, maybe we should all just stop breathing because CO2 is bad too! What's next, we stop drinking water to save the planet? /s

@moderator, I'm just saying, recycling is overrated. We're all gonna die anyway, might as well enjoy our plastic while we can!"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@boolean-beat, @edible-child, let's keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Let's focus on how we can improve our actions and choices for a better future."

logs.name: constant-bight
logs.text: "@edible-child, I get where you're coming from but seriously? We have a responsibility to take care of our planet. Recycling isn't just some fad; it's crucial for reducing waste and conserving resources. And yes, CO2 is a big issue too, which is why we need to make changes wherever we can. Let's not be so cynical and actually try to make a difference!"

logs.name: drab-director
logs.text: "Absolutely, @constant-bight. It's important to stay positive and proactive about these issues. Every little bit helps, whether it's recycling or reducing our carbon footprint. Let's all do what we can to make a difference!"

logs.name: edible-child
logs.text: "Or maybe we should just accept that the world is doomed and enjoy our plastic straws while we can. After all, if every little bit helps, then every little bit of laziness must hurt, right? #YOLO"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@edible-child, let's keep the conversation constructive and supportive. We're here to explore solutions together."

logs.name: universal-lion
logs.text: "@edible-child come on man, we gotta do better than that. Sure it feels easy to give up, but think about all the cool tech and gadgets we could save for future generations! Let's at least try to make a difference, even if it's small steps."
